metadata.title:
ANOTHER BRIDGE FARCE


EXHIBITION Que TONIGHT

w. W. p. Ready With Old Promises and Devices


to Delay Demand for Expenditure by Company.

pages.raw_text:
Following a meeting of Mayor
Moore, councilmen and representa-
tives of the Washington Water
Power Co. yesterday afternoon,
Mayor Moore has called for spe-
cial meeting of the council tonight
at which the objection Of the. Wash
ington Water Power Co. to the
Wallace-Coates bridge plans will be
presented.


1t is just possible that the coun-
Gil may exact from Henry M.
Richards, president of the w. w. p,
another promise about paying part
of the cost Of new bridges. The
same promise" was made In re-
gard to the Howard st. span but it
was never kept.


The council, mayor then in, Office
and every city Official having au-
thority, is just as much to blame
for that as Richards, however, for
no effort was made to compel good
faith. They allowed him to pull
the wool over their eyes with his
promises and said they liked 1t.


Judging from this, after the
bridge question is finally threshed
out, and everyone has had a say,
the city will pay all the construc-
ton price for the new bridges, the
w. w. p. will use them and per-
haps knock them down some more,
and the taxper will continue to
howl, as in the past. The meeting
tonight will be farcical in that 1t
bears the aspect of requesting
